title: day3 30
caption: Pic 21
Pic 1: View from the outside of Belzec, one of many Nazi death camps where Jews were sent to be exterminated
Pic 2: Path that Jews would walk on as they approached the gas chambers in Belzec
Pic 3: View from the end of the path that Jews would walk on as they approached the gas chambers in Belzec
Pic 4: Memorial to the Jews lost in Belzec, as well as a call to action
Pic 5: The rubble of Belzec, which was left there so that the camp’s existence and the atrocities committed couldn’t be erased and forgotten
Pic 6: Railroad tracks stacked on top of each other that were part of the railroad that brought Jews to Belzec
Pic 7: View of shoes within the Belzec museum that were taken from the Jews processed and killed there
Pic 8: View of moat surrounding Zamość Fortress
Pic 9: View down a tunnel connected Zamość Fortress
Pic 10: View down a street of Zamość
Pic 11: View down another street of Zamość
Pic 12: Torah scroll left behind by traveling Jews during the Nazis advance that was eventually donated to a synagogue (might need to correct me I cant remember exactly)
Pic 13: Memorial of Allan Gerson, son of Holocaust survivors and attorney.
Pic 14: View of Torah scroll within a synagogue in Zamość
Pic 15: Buildings in the Great Market Square in Zamość
Pic 16: Zamość Town Hall
Pic 17: View down a street in the Great Market Square in Zamość
Pic 18: (I don’t know the context of this statue) next to the Parish of the Resurrection of the Lord and St. Thomas the Apostle in Zamość
Pic 19: Statue of St. John Paul II, who was pope from 1978 to 2005, outside of the Parish of the Resurrection of the Lord and St. Thomas the Apostle in Zamość
Pic 20: View of the sunset near the Zamość Fortress
